title: Community Organising
shortSummary: Build grassroots power from the ground up — recruit volunteers, mobilise communities, run local campaigns, and create lasting political movements that outlive any single election.
whyItMatters: The most durable political power comes from organised communities, not individual candidates. Community organising is how movements win — from local council issues to national policy change. It is the engine beneath every successful political operation.
coreConcepts: Power mapping and community asset identification
